summary:
In patients with traumatic rib injuries presenting with respiratory distress, tachypnea, and hemodynamic instability, tension pneumothorax must be ruled out urgently because it causes sudden cardiovascular collapse. Immediate needle decompression at the second intercostal space mid-clavicular line is critical when such findings suggest an obstructed intrathoracic pressure—this prevents fatal progression while awaiting definitive diagnosis through radiographic confirmation.

The summary above contains more than two sentences; please revise strictly within exactly two concise sentences focusing solely on core clinical actions:

Patient has significant trauma-related risks including tension pneumothorax based on multi-rib fracture, tachypnea, and hypotension. Next immediate step is needle decompression at the second intercostal space mid-clavicular line to relieve acute cardiac tamponade-like obstruction from elevated intrapleural pressure.
